stories.headline: Clarity Act faces Memorial Day cliff as 100+ crypto firms storm Senate
stories.tldr: A coalition of over 100 crypto firms is pressing Senate Banking to mark up the Clarity Act before a May 25 cutoff for 2026 passage.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Miss the window and the U.S. spends another year arbitraging crypto via SEC enforcement instead of statute, freezing institutional product launches.
the_details: Coinbase, Ripple and 100+ firms signed a letter urging Banking Chair Tim Scott to schedule a markup.
Sen. Thom Tillis told reporters no April hearing; Banking is now eyeing May for amendments and a vote.
Memorial Day, May 25, has been the industry's drop-dead date for legislation to clear before the election.

stories.headline: April becomes worst month for crypto hacks since Bybit, $606M lost in 18 days
stories.tldr: Crypto protocols lost more than $606 million across 12 incidents in the first 18 days of April, the worst stretch since the $1.4B Bybit breach.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: A wave of cross-chain bridge and perp-DEX exploits is rerating DeFi risk premia just as the Clarity Act needs a clean industry narrative.
the_details: Kelp DAO drained for $292M on April 19 via a LayerZero bridge flaw, stranding wrapped ether across 20 chains.
Drift Protocol on Solana lost $285M on April 1, briefly the year's biggest hack until Kelp eclipsed it.
Aave saw $8.45B in deposit outflows after $200M of stolen Kelp tokens were posted as collateral.

stories.headline: BlackRock IBIT options open interest tops Deribit, marking U.S. derivatives flip
stories.tldr: BlackRock's spot bitcoin ETF IBIT logged options open interest above Deribit's, the first time a U.S.-regulated venue has crossed crypto's largest offshore book.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Listed BTC volatility is migrating onshore, giving institutions a regulated hedging rail and pulling fee revenue away from offshore venues.
the_details: IBIT options OI surpassed Deribit on Friday, per market data cited by CoinDesk.
BTC trades near $79,123, up 2% in 24h with $24.7B volume; Fear & Greed back to 47 from 29 last week.
Bitcoin futures OI fell 6% to 744.3K BTC as spot ran into resistance below $80,000.

stories.headline: SEC clears DEX front-ends from broker-dealer registration in April 13 staff note
stories.tldr: SEC staff said it will not object to software interfaces that prepare and submit transactions in crypto asset securities without broker-dealer registration.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: It removes a years-old enforcement threat over DEX UIs and aggregators, unblocking U.S.-hosted decentralized trading frontends.
the_details: The April 13, 2026 staff statement applies to interfaces that don't custody assets or quote prices.
It builds on the joint SEC-CFTC interpretive release from March 17 that set a five-bucket token taxonomy.
Conditions still apply, including no order-routing for value and no exercise of discretion.

stories.headline: Solana's Alpenglow ships sub-200ms finality as Firedancer aims for 1M TPS
stories.tldr: Solana's Alpenglow upgrade is live, slashing transaction finality below 200ms, while Firedancer continues targeting 1M TPS as ETF inflows slump.
stories.why_it_matters: Why it matters: Solana is doubling down on throughput as institutional flow cools, betting payments rails (Mastercard, Visa partners) outpace ETF demand.
the_details: Alpenglow finality now under 200 ms on mainnet; Firedancer client targets 1M TPS once fully rolled out.
SOL trades near $86 with a $49.8B cap; spot SOL ETF inflows are down 92% from late-2025 highs.
Solana Developer Platform, launched March 24, already lists Mastercard, Worldpay, and Western Union as users.
